Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 184 upvotes, 22 comments
  2. Paper Tape Is All You Need – Training a Transformer on a 1976 Minicomputer 80 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. Nashville library launches Memory Lab for digitizing home movies 181 upvotes, 43 comments
  4. Matadisco – Decentralized Data Discovery 41 upvotes, 6 comments
  5. Militarized snowflakes: The accidental beauty of Renaissance star forts 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. Spanish legislation as a Git repo 541 upvotes, 164 comments
  7. StationeryObject 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. The bee that everyone wants to save 205 upvotes, 67 comments
  9. Cocoa-Way – Native macOS Wayland compositor for running Linux apps seamlessly 202 upvotes, 66 comments
  10. I decompiled the White House's new app 85 upvotes, 24 comments
  11. Arm releases first in-house chip, with Meta as debut customer 69 upvotes, 19 comments
  12. C++26: A User-Friednly assert() macro 27 upvotes, 5 comments
  13. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 553 upvotes, 240 comments
  14. Gerard of Cremona 21 upvotes, 5 comments
  15. CERN uses tiny AI models burned into silicon for real-time LHC data filtering 222 upvotes, 108 comments
  16. LG's new 1Hz display is the secret behind a new laptop's battery life 291 upvotes, 147 comments
  17. Improved Git Diffs with Delta, Fzf and a Little Shell Scripting 49 upvotes, 18 comments
  18. Go hard on agents, not on your filesystem 506 upvotes, 277 comments
  19. AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition crams 208MB of cache into a single chip 246 upvotes, 130 comments
  20. Show HN: Free, in-browser PDF editor 26 upvotes, 9 comments
  21. Britain today generating 90%+ of electricity from renewables 357 upvotes, 230 comments
  22. Folk are getting dangerously attached to AI that always tells them they're right 115 upvotes, 68 comments
  23. Make macOS consistently bad unironically 476 upvotes, 330 comments
  24. Go Naming Conventions: A Practical Guide 60 upvotes, 34 comments
  25. ICAO issued new power bank restriction on flight 22 upvotes, 9 comments
  26. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 265 upvotes, 194 comments
  27. A single-file C allocator with explicit heaps and tuning knobs 43 upvotes, 23 comments
  28. Iran-linked hackers breach FBI director's personal email 371 upvotes, 479 comments
  29. We built a multi-agent research hub. The waitlist is a reverse-CAPTCHA 8 upvotes, 7 comments
  30. Toma (YC W24) is hiring a Senior/Staff Eng to build AI automotive coworkers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 29 March 2026. Source here.
